rationale: Neither candidate directly surfaces the ground-truth reviewer's narrow question about verifying reduce_scatter coverage in the test, though X's validated notes at least engage with that exact test detail and X independently catches a real, grounded gap (missing cfg_parallel_size=1 test coverage) that echoes the same 'did the two fixes get jointly verified' concern. X gives more concrete, line-specific, copy-pasteable suggestions (docstring text, comment wording, test case to add), which raises actionability despite some findings being low-value nitpicks. Y is more rigorously verified (checked DPMetadata dataclass fields, destroy_model_parallel cleanup, use_device_communicator default) and stays cleaner/higher-precision, but ends in a bare APPROVE with only one soft, non-actionable comment, contributing less reviewable substance.
